tweets.text:
BitMEX changed my life forever, grateful to have met the founders and consider them friends.

Many mistakes were made, but they played a pivotal role in the industry and the founders success is well deserved. 

A true story of novel implementation, great marketing and execution minting a trio of billionaires.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Dear BitMEX Users,

Today, we share with a very heavy heart that BitMEX exchange will shut down its operations, effective 23 September 2026 at 04:00:00 UTC.

The owner and operator of BitMEX, HDR Global Trading Limited, has made the difficult decision to close operations https://t.co/oWuqlh547f

thread_tweets.full_text:
BitMEX changed my life forever, grateful to have met the founders and consider them friends.

Many mistakes were made, but they played a pivotal role in the industry and the founders success is well deserved. 

A true story of novel implementation, great marketing and execution minting a trio of billionaires.

thread_tweets.text:
A lot of newfags aren't aware of BitMEX, or the sheer magnitude of the exchange pre 2020. There was a period of time where BitMEX had over 80% market share for perpetual swaps, and nearly 90% of the open interest.

A lot of the crypto market structure we have today is due to a result of realtime testing on BitMEX.

Important to celebrate the fact BitMEX has never had a major security indecent, and largely has been a net positive for Bitcoin adoption over the years!

Impossible to argue they didn't propel this industry forward!

thread_tweets.text:
Favorite and final part of this saga was the FBI showing up at my door due to erroneously suspecting that I was an employee there. 

Never was, but tried to buy it with some people a few years back!

tweets.full_text:
BitMEX changed my life forever, grateful to have met the founders and consider them friends.

Many mistakes were made, but they played a pivotal role in the industry and the founders success is well deserved. 

A true story of novel implementation, great marketing and execution minting a trio of billionaires.

A lot of newfags aren't aware of BitMEX, or the sheer magnitude of the exchange pre 2020. There was a period of time where BitMEX had over 80% market share for perpetual swaps, and nearly 90% of the open interest.

A lot of the crypto market structure we have today is due to a result of realtime testing on BitMEX.

Important to celebrate the fact BitMEX has never had a major security indecent, and largely has been a net positive for Bitcoin adoption over the years!

Impossible to argue they didn't propel this industry forward!

Favorite and final part of this saga was the FBI showing up at my door due to erroneously suspecting that I was an employee there. 

Never was, but tried to buy it with some people a few years back!

tweets.text:
gm

GME pumped last night on Robinhood Chain because it was paired with GME (the RWA).

Cool idea - buying the meme forces people to buy the RWA token, which theoretically increases demand in both and everyone wins.

Only issue is RWA have a real lack of liquidity on Robinhood atm - so when there are heavy demand events… there isn’t always enough supply that exists on chain to move the stock token in parity with what its value is on the market. This creates an arb opportunity for anyone who can mint the RWA on chain.

The “demand” created by the meme at these market caps and volume is a blip compared to  what the RWA stock actually trades.

So - we’re at the stage in Robinhood where any popular RWA asset pairs are very easy up, but should also reflexively be very easy down.

Honestly wouldn’t be surprised to see a few more RWA pairs squeeze today before things calm down (almost like aftershocks of an earthquake).

But - even though the “squeeze both” idea might not be incredibly valid today… there’s a real possibility it can be in the future when there is more volume and liquidity.

And in the meantime, it’s one hell of a marketing event to get people to bridge to Robinhood Chain.

So even though a lot of these pairs will correct sharply when the initial hype dies - the narrative and the momentum on chain is real and just getting started.

Still early.

GME pumped last night on Robinhood Chain because it was paired with GME (the RWA).

Cool idea - buying the meme forces people to buy the RWA token, which theoretically increases demand in both and everyone wins.

Only issue is RWA have a real lack of liquidity on Robinhood atm - so when there are heavy demand events… there isn’t always enough supply that exists on chain to move the stock token in parity with what its value is on the market. This creates an arb opportunity for anyone who can mint the RWA on chain.

The “demand” created by the meme at these market caps and volume is a blip compared to  what the RWA stock actually trades.

So - we’re at the stage in Robinhood where any popular RWA asset pairs are very easy up, but should also reflexively be very easy down.

Honestly wouldn’t be surprised to see a few more RWA pairs squeeze today before things calm down (almost like aftershocks of an earthquake).

But - even though the “squeeze both” idea might not be incredibly valid today… there’s a real possibility it can be in the future when there is more volume and liquidity.

tweets.text: Once again, traders are getting spooked by the rise in bond yields. But the breakdown in bonds is actually bullish for gold, as it reflects soaring federal budget deficits, out-of-control inflation, and a loss of confidence in the creditworthiness of the U.S. Buy the dip!
tweets.full_text: Once again, traders are getting spooked by the rise in bond yields. But the breakdown in bonds is actually bullish for gold, as it reflects soaring federal budget deficits, out-of-control inflation, and a loss of confidence in the creditworthiness of the U.S. Buy the dip!

tweets.text: The yield on the 30-year Treasury is 5.18%, its highest since April 2006. At that time, the U.S. national debt was $8.35 trillion. Now it’s $39.6 trillion, almost five times as large. The U.S. can’t afford these rates, let alone the much higher rates we'll soon be forced to pay.
tweets.full_text: The yield on the 30-year Treasury is 5.18%, its highest since April 2006. At that time, the U.S. national debt was $8.35 trillion. Now it’s $39.6 trillion, almost five times as large. The U.S. can’t afford these rates, let alone the much higher rates we'll soon be forced to pay.

tweets.text: Investors celebrated the June CPI, as a 30% fall in the price of oil led to a larger-than-expected decline. But so far in July, the price of oil is already up 30%, back above $90 per barrel. If the price hits $100 by month-end, that will be a 43% rise. July CPI could be a doozy!

thread_tweets.text: Investors celebrated the June CPI, as a 30% fall in the price of oil led to a larger-than-expected decline. But so far in July, the price of oil is already up 30%, back above $90 per barrel. If the price hits $100 by month-end, that will be a 43% rise. July CPI could be a doozy!
thread_tweets.full_text: Investors celebrated the June CPI, as a 30% fall in the price of oil led to a larger-than-expected decline. But so far in July, the price of oil is already up 30%, back above $90 per barrel. If the price hits $100 by month-end, that will be a 43% rise. July CPI could be a doozy!

thread_tweets.text: @CoinEdition No, it's just that the only reason June CPI fell so much was the 30% drop in oil. That will likely be completely reversed by an even bigger rise in the price of oil in July.
thread_tweets.full_text: @CoinEdition No, it's just that the only reason June CPI fell so much was the 30% drop in oil. That will likely be completely reversed by an even bigger rise in the price of oil in July.

tweets.full_text:
Investors celebrated the June CPI, as a 30% fall in the price of oil led to a larger-than-expected decline. But so far in July, the price of oil is already up 30%, back above $90 per barrel. If the price hits $100 by month-end, that will be a 43% rise. July CPI could be a doozy!

@CoinEdition No, it's just that the only reason June CPI fell so much was the 30% drop in oil. That will likely be completely reversed by an even bigger rise in the price of oil in July.

tweets.text:
i've been rebuilding my trading platform from when i used to trade equities.

main things that i'm building out for myself include it being lighter weight, hotkey trading, and on mac.

starting with the basicas of what i used to have, but quickly adding stuff i wish i had at the time.

it's been incredibly fun to do.
